title: Adding Dollar Sign to formula column
excerpt: I created a formula column and I want to display the dollar sign in the column.  I used the text option for the dollar sign but when I did that, only one of my formula columns worked and the other one is showing an error…

posts.author: nado2oo2 (Scott)
posts.cookedHtml: I created a formula column and I want to display the dollar sign in the column. I used the text option for the dollar sign but when I did that, only one of my formula columns worked and the other one is showing an error. Is there a way to automatically have the $ present like I can do with the number column? Thanks your help!

posts.cookedHtml:

posts.author: janelegall (Jane)
posts.cookedHtml: I am trying to added a dollar sign to my formula, but I am confused with how the TEXT part sits within the formula. My formula is a simple subtraction of two number columns, and I need the result to have a dollar sign. Can someone share with me how I would write this forumula?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@janelegall

Basically:

TEXT({Num1}-{Num2},“$#,##0.00”)

Jim - Subscribe to The Monday Man

posts.author: Kenny (noneofyourbusiness)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JCorrell

Am I right, that this is only working when using $? It simply removes the currency sign when using €.
How can I use € instead?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Kenny

When possible, the preferable method is to simply do the required calculation then set the unit by clicking into the column summary (if you don’t have the column summary displayed, add it back temporarily).

This is not always possible. To use TEXT() formatting function with a different currency symbol do something like this:

"€" & TEXT({Num1}-{Num2}, "#,##0.00")

It’s not perfect. The formatting for numeric values in formula columns could do with some upgrades.

posts.author: Kenny (noneofyourbusiness)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JCorrell
I can live with that. Thank you very much!

posts.author: rachelmele (Rachel Mele)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, this is my formula IF({Stage} = “Verbal”, SUM({Value}), " ") How would I add the $ to this formula? Thanks.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@rachelmele

Here are 2 examples:
IF({Stage} = "Verbal", "$" & SUM({Value}), "")
IF({Stage} = "Verbal", TEXT(SUM({Value}), "$#,##0.00"), "")

posts.author: cberman (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml: Actually monday has built-in support to show the unit, you don’t need any TEXT in your formula. Just go to the column summary at the bottom and set the unit! It seems like something that should be at the top under column settings, but it is in the footer.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@rachelmele

@cberman is correct. That is definitely the preferred method! (As I wrote in the Mar 3 post.)

However, it doesn’t work in every situation.

posts.author: ashley_unltd (Ash)
posts.cookedHtml: Using the TEXT function works for a singular formula column but if I try to add two similar formula columns together it errors (I assume becase it now treats them as TEXT columns?)

posts.author: ashley_unltd (Ash)
posts.cookedHtml: Using the TEXT function works for a singular formula column but if I try to add two similar formula columns together it errors (I assume becase it now treats them as TEXT columns?)

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@ashley_unltd

One way is to reproduce the formulas that you want to add in the new formula:
For example:
{formula 1} = 1/2
{formula 2} = {number} * 3
{formula 3} = (1+2) + ({number * 3)

Another is to use SUBSTITUTE() to remove the extraneous characters:
from above… {formula 3} = SUBSTITUTE(SUBSTITUTE({formlula 1}, "\$", ""), ",", "") + SUBSTITUTE(SUBSTITUTE({formlula 2}, "\$", ""), ",", "")

OR

SUBSTITUTE({formlula 1}, "[\$,]", "") + SUBSTITUTE({formlula 2}, "[\$,]", "")

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)

posts.author: Junius (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Jim, here’s another one I’d appreciate your help with.

If I’m simply multiplying one column by another and want a $ sign in the formula column what would I add to:
MULTIPLY({ACV},{Years}

Thanks.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Junius

Something like this…
TEXT(MULTIPLY({ACV},{Years}), "$#,##0.00"), "")

posts.author: Grimmvg (Grimm Van Gestel)
posts.cookedHtml:
I can’t find a solution for my issue either. I got 2 columns , one {Qty} one {Sales Price} , and another column with a dropdown menu with different currencies we operate ($‚€,…)

Im trying to make a formula that multiplies Qty*Sales prices, and add the string value of the dropdown menu so i can dynamically change the outputted currency on the value in a quote builder tool.

Thanks !

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Grimmvg

Try something like this:
{dropdown} & ({Qty} * {Sales Price})

Jim - The Monday Man

posts.author: kcarpenter (Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you!! The AI said use ‘FORMAT_NUMBER’ which wasn’t working but this did like a champ!

Here is how i used it: TEXT({Cost}*{Quantity},“$0.00”)
